name: The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel
name_original: El Bosque de Las Blancas — Circular desde Puebla de San Miguel
region_intro: Hiking around Puebla De San Miguel, a municipality designated as a Natural Park in Valencia, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by ancient juniper forests, notably in Las Blancas, and features numerous yew trees found in ravines like Ripoll and La Hoz. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from gentle paths to the challenging ascent of Cerro Calderón, the highest peak in the Valencian Community at 1,838 meters. The area also includes several natural springs and offers a network of trails within its protected natural environment. Best hiking trails around Puebla De San Miguel The most popular hiking route is Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route features waterfalls and diverse terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. This trail leads through the ancient juniper forest, home to monumental trees. Local hikers also love the Puebla de San Miguel Village – Las Blancas (Juniper Forest) loop from Puebla de San Miguel, a 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail leading through ancient juniper woodlands, often completed in about 5 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Puebla De San Miguel is defined by ancient juniper forests, deep ravines, and the high peaks of the Valencian Community. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous ascents. The routes in Puebla De San Miguel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from 16 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De San Miguel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Puebla de San Miguel?
A: Puebla de San Miguel offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 9 easy, 15 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Puebla de San Miguel has 9 easy hiking routes perfect for families or beginners. These trails generally feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the park. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the overall easy category is a good starting point.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many trails in Puebla de San Miguel are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, you can explore the ancient juniper forest on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822450801/the-forest-of-las-blancas-circular-from-puebla-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, or take the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de San Miguel Village – Las Blancas (Juniper Forest) loop from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers in Puebla de San Miguel can discover a variety of unique natural features. A major highlight is the ancient juniper forest, home to monumental junipers estimated to be up to 2,000 years old, which you can visit via &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Blancas (Juniper Forest)&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts numerous yew trees, particularly in the Ripoll Ravine. You'll also find several natural springs, such as El Pozo and La Canaleja, offering fresh water along the way.

Q: Are there any waterfalls in Puebla de San Miguel?
A: Yes, the region features beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974593688/amanaderos-de-riodeva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.3-mile moderate route specifically known for its waterfalls and diverse terrain. You can also find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; as a notable highlight.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike in the area?
A: The highest point you can hike to in the Valencian Community, located within the Natural Park, is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderón (Alto de las Barracas)&lt;/a&gt;, standing at 1,838 meters. This peak offers stunning panoramic views and is a significant attraction for more challenging hikes.

Q: Are there any shelters or refuges available for hikers?
A: Yes, for those undertaking longer or more challenging hikes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicente Pine – Collado del Buey Shelter loop from Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Refugio Collado del Buey. Located at 1,720 meters, this basic refuge can serve as a convenient stop, especially for hikers heading towards Pico Calderón.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Puebla de San Miguel?
A: The trails in Puebla de San Miguel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from 16 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique ancient juniper forests, the diverse landscapes, and the well-preserved natural environment of the Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Puebla de San Miguel?
A: Puebla de San Miguel offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with vibrant flora in spring and beautiful colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter hikes, particularly to higher peaks like Cerro Calderón, may require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

Q: Are there specific trails that showcase the ancient juniper forests?
A: Absolutely. The ancient juniper forests, particularly in the area of Las Blancas, are a major highlight. You can explore these monumental trees on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822450801/the-forest-of-las-blancas-circular-from-puebla-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt; or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de San Miguel Village – Las Blancas (Juniper Forest) loop from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Puebla de San Miguel is a Natural Park, and while many natural areas in Spain permit dogs on trails, it's always recommended to check the specific regulations of the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park before your visit. Generally, dogs should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to significant viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: Many trails in Puebla de San Miguel offer scenic views, especially those that ascend to higher elevations. The hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderón (Alto de las Barracas)&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Valencian Community, provides exceptional panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural park and beyond.
